questions.prompt: A student leaves a sealed jar of water in the freezer overnight. In the morning, the water has become solid ice and the jar has cracked. Which statement best explains what happened to the water particles?
choices: The particles disappeared and the empty space pushed against the jar.
The particles slowed down, locked into a fixed pattern, and expanded slightly so the ice took up more volume than the liquid.
The particles sped up and pressed against the walls of the jar until it broke.
The particles changed into a different substance with a larger mass.
questions.correctText: The particles slowed down, locked into a fixed pattern, and expanded slightly so the ice took up more volume than the liquid.
questions.explanation: When water freezes, the particles lose kinetic energy and lock into an open hexagonal crystal pattern that takes up about 9% more volume than liquid water. Mass stays the same; only the arrangement and spacing of particles change.

questions.prompt: Which row correctly identifies an atom, a molecule, and a compound?
choices: Atom = H2O, Molecule = O, Compound = O2
Atom = O, Molecule = O2, Compound = H2O
Atom = O2, Molecule = H2O, Compound = O
Atom = H2O, Molecule = O2, Compound = O
questions.correctText: Atom = O, Molecule = O2, Compound = H2O
questions.explanation: An atom is a single particle of one element (O). A molecule is two or more atoms bonded together (O2 is two oxygens). A compound is a molecule made of two or more different elements (H2O has hydrogen and oxygen).

questions.prompt: Which observation is evidence that a chemical change has occurred, rather than a physical change?
choices: An ice cube melts into a puddle of water on a warm counter.
A piece of aluminum foil is folded into a small square.
A nail left outside for weeks develops a flaky reddish-brown coating of rust.
Sugar dissolves into a glass of warm tea.
questions.correctText: A nail left outside for weeks develops a flaky reddish-brown coating of rust.
questions.explanation: Rusting produces a new substance (iron oxide) with different properties than iron, which is a chemical change. Melting, folding, and dissolving change form but not the identity of the substance.

questions.prompt: A student measures a small rock and finds a mass of 60 grams. When the rock is placed in a graduated cylinder containing 40 mL of water, the water level rises to 60 mL. What is the density of the rock?
choices: 1.0 g/mL
3.0 g/mL
20 g/mL
questions.correctText: 3.0 g/mL
questions.explanation: Volume by displacement = 60 mL - 40 mL = 20 mL. Density = mass / volume = 60 g / 20 mL = 3.0 g/mL. Because this is greater than water's density of 1.0 g/mL, the rock would sink.

questions.prompt: A skateboarder is at the very top of a half-pipe, momentarily not moving, just before rolling down. As the skateboarder rolls from the top to the bottom of the ramp, what happens to their energy?
choices: Kinetic energy converts into gravitational potential energy as they go down.
Gravitational potential energy converts into kinetic energy as they speed up.
Both kinetic and potential energy decrease, and the energy is destroyed.
Potential energy stays constant while kinetic energy is created from nothing.
questions.correctText: Gravitational potential energy converts into kinetic energy as they speed up.
questions.explanation: At the top, the skateboarder has maximum gravitational potential energy (because of height) and minimum kinetic energy. As they roll down, height decreases and speed increases, transforming potential energy into kinetic energy. Total mechanical energy is conserved (ignoring friction).

questions.prompt: A metal spoon is placed in a mug of hot cocoa. After a few minutes, the handle of the spoon, which is sticking out of the cocoa, also feels hot. Which method of heat transfer best explains why the handle warmed up?
choices: Convection, because hot air rises around the spoon.
Radiation, because the cocoa emits infrared waves through the air.
Conduction, because energy moves through the metal by particle collisions.
Evaporation, because steam from the cocoa heats the metal.
questions.correctText: Conduction, because energy moves through the metal by particle collisions.
questions.explanation: Conduction is heat transfer through direct contact, when faster-moving particles bump into slower ones and pass energy along. Metals are good conductors, so heat moves up the spoon from the hot end to the cool handle.

questions.prompt: Two guitar strings are plucked. String 1 vibrates 200 times per second. String 2 vibrates 400 times per second. Both strings are plucked with the same force, so they have the same amplitude. Which statement is correct?
choices: String 2 has a higher pitch but the same volume as String 1.
String 1 has a higher pitch but a lower volume than String 2.
Both strings have the same pitch but String 2 is louder.
String 2 has a lower pitch and a higher volume than String 1.
questions.correctText: String 2 has a higher pitch but the same volume as String 1.
questions.explanation: Frequency (vibrations per second) determines pitch — higher frequency means higher pitch. Amplitude determines volume (loudness). Same amplitude means same volume, while doubled frequency means a higher pitch.

questions.prompt: White light from a flashlight is shined through a glass prism, and a rainbow appears on the wall behind it. Which statement best explains why this happens?
choices: The prism adds new colors to the light that were not there before.
White light is made of all the visible colors, and the prism refracts each color by a different amount.
The prism reflects only the visible colors and absorbs the rest.
The glass converts heat from the flashlight into colored light.
questions.correctText: White light is made of all the visible colors, and the prism refracts each color by a different amount.
questions.explanation: White light contains the full visible spectrum. When light enters a prism, it bends (refracts), and each color bends by a slightly different angle because of its wavelength. This separates the colors into the familiar red-to-violet rainbow.

questions.prompt: A soccer ball is sitting still on the field. A player runs up and kicks it, and the ball flies forward. Which of Newton's laws best explains why the ball stayed at rest until the player kicked it?
choices: Newton's First Law: an object at rest stays at rest unless acted on by an unbalanced force.
Newton's Second Law: force equals mass times acceleration.
Newton's Third Law: for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.
Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation.
questions.correctText: Newton's First Law: an object at rest stays at rest unless acted on by an unbalanced force.
questions.explanation: Newton's First Law (the law of inertia) says objects at rest stay at rest unless an unbalanced force acts on them. The ball sat still until the kick provided that unbalanced force.

questions.prompt: Two students are pulling on opposite ends of a rope. Student A pulls with 80 newtons to the left. Student B pulls with 80 newtons to the right. Which best describes the rope's motion and the forces on it?
choices: The forces are balanced and the rope does not move.
The forces are unbalanced and the rope accelerates to the left.
The forces are unbalanced and the rope accelerates to the right.
There are no forces acting on the rope because they cancel and disappear.
questions.correctText: The forces are balanced and the rope does not move.
questions.explanation: Equal forces in opposite directions are balanced; the net force is 0 N. With no unbalanced force, the rope has no acceleration and stays at rest. The forces are still acting — they just cancel each other out.

questions.prompt: A student slides a heavy box across a carpeted floor. The box slows down and eventually stops, even though the student is no longer pushing it. Which force is most directly responsible for the box slowing to a stop?
choices: Gravity pulling the box downward through the floor.
Friction between the box and the carpet acting opposite to the motion.
Magnetism between the box and the floor.
Air resistance pushing the box sideways.
questions.correctText: Friction between the box and the carpet acting opposite to the motion.
questions.explanation: Friction is a force between two surfaces in contact that opposes motion. The rough carpet creates a large friction force pointing opposite to the box's direction of travel, slowing the box until its speed reaches zero.

questions.prompt: A cyclist travels 150 meters in 30 seconds at a steady pace. A distance-time graph of her trip from start to finish would look like which of the following?
choices: A horizontal flat line at 150 m.
A curve that gets steeper over time.
A straight line sloping upward at a constant rate, with an average speed of 5 m/s.
A line sloping downward, showing she is moving backward at 5 m/s.
questions.correctText: A straight line sloping upward at a constant rate, with an average speed of 5 m/s.
questions.explanation: Speed = distance / time = 150 m / 30 s = 5 m/s. A constant speed produces a straight, upward-sloping line on a distance-time graph; the slope of that line equals the speed.

questions.topic: Life Sciences: Cells and Life Processes
questions.prompt: A student is looking at a plant cell under a microscope. Which structure would she see in this plant cell that would NOT be found in a typical animal cell?
choices: Nucleus
Cell membrane
Mitochondria
Cell wall and chloroplasts
questions.correctText: Cell wall and chloroplasts
questions.explanation: All eukaryotic cells have a nucleus, cell membrane, and mitochondria. Plant cells are distinguished by a rigid cell wall (made of cellulose) outside the cell membrane and by chloroplasts that carry out photosynthesis. Animal cells have neither.

questions.topic: Life Sciences: Cells and Life Processes
questions.prompt: Which sequence correctly lists the levels of organization in a multicellular organism, from smallest to largest?
choices: Organ -> tissue -> cell -> organ system -> organism
Cell -> tissue -> organ -> organ system -> organism
Cell -> organ -> tissue -> organism -> organ system
Tissue -> cell -> organism -> organ -> organ system
questions.correctText: Cell -> tissue -> organ -> organ system -> organism
questions.explanation: Cells of the same type form tissues. Different tissues working together form an organ (such as the heart). Organs working together form an organ system (such as the circulatory system). All the systems together form the organism.

questions.topic: Life Sciences: Cells and Life Processes
questions.prompt:
A student writes two word equations on the board:
Equation 1: carbon dioxide + water -> glucose + oxygen (using light)
Equation 2: glucose + oxygen -> carbon dioxide + water (releases energy)
Which statement best describes these two processes?
choices: Both equations describe photosynthesis.
Both equations describe cellular respiration.
Equation 1 is photosynthesis and Equation 2 is cellular respiration; together they cycle matter and energy.
Equation 1 is digestion and Equation 2 is excretion.
questions.correctText: Equation 1 is photosynthesis and Equation 2 is cellular respiration; together they cycle matter and energy.
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis uses light energy to build glucose from CO2 and water, releasing oxygen. Cellular respiration breaks glucose down using oxygen, releasing CO2, water, and usable energy. The two processes are essentially reverses of each other and cycle matter through ecosystems.

questions.topic: Life Sciences: Cells and Life Processes
questions.prompt: A strawberry plant sends out a runner that grows roots and becomes a new plant identical to the parent. Which statement best describes this method of reproduction?
choices: It is sexual reproduction, and the offspring have a mix of two parents' genes.
It is asexual reproduction, and the offspring are genetically identical clones of the single parent.
It is asexual reproduction, but each offspring is genetically unique.
It is sexual reproduction because two cells fuse to make the new plant.
questions.correctText: It is asexual reproduction, and the offspring are genetically identical clones of the single parent.
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction involves only one parent and produces offspring that are genetic copies (clones). Runners, bulbs, and cuttings are common in plants. Sexual reproduction requires two parents and creates genetic variation.

questions.prompt: A simple food chain in a meadow ecosystem is: grass -> grasshopper -> frog -> hawk. If the meadow contains 10,000 units of energy stored in grass, which estimate best describes how much energy is available to the hawk at the top of the chain?
choices: About 10,000 units, because energy is conserved at every level.
About 1,000 units, because each level keeps most of the energy.
About 10 units, because roughly 90% of energy is lost as heat at each transfer.
About 0 units, because energy cannot move through a food chain.
questions.correctText: About 10 units, because roughly 90% of energy is lost as heat at each transfer.
questions.explanation: Energy pyramids show that only about 10% of the energy at one level is passed to the next; the rest is lost as heat or used for life processes. Starting with 10,000 units in grass, the grasshopper has about 1,000, the frog about 100, and the hawk only about 10.

questions.prompt: Mushrooms, certain bacteria, and worms feed on dead leaves on a forest floor. What is the most important role these organisms play in the ecosystem?
choices: They are producers that create their own food using sunlight.
They are decomposers that break down dead material and return nutrients to the soil.
They are top predators that control the populations of larger animals.
They are abiotic factors that have no living function in the ecosystem.
questions.correctText: They are decomposers that break down dead material and return nutrients to the soil.
questions.explanation: Decomposers feed on dead plant and animal matter, breaking it down chemically. They recycle nutrients like nitrogen and carbon back into the soil so plants (producers) can absorb them again. Without decomposers, nutrients would be locked in dead matter.

questions.prompt: A scientist studying a pond ecosystem lists the following factors: water temperature, dissolved oxygen, fish, sunlight, algae, rocks, ducks, and pH of water. Which list correctly separates these into abiotic and biotic factors?
choices: Abiotic: fish, algae, ducks | Biotic: water temperature, sunlight, rocks, pH, dissolved oxygen
Abiotic: water temperature, dissolved oxygen, sunlight, rocks, pH | Biotic: fish, algae, ducks
Abiotic: fish, ducks | Biotic: algae, rocks, sunlight
Abiotic: algae, ducks, sunlight | Biotic: fish, pH, dissolved oxygen, water temperature, rocks
questions.correctText: Abiotic: water temperature, dissolved oxygen, sunlight, rocks, pH | Biotic: fish, algae, ducks
questions.explanation: Biotic factors are living things or parts that were once alive (fish, algae, ducks). Abiotic factors are nonliving physical and chemical features (temperature, oxygen, sunlight, rocks, pH). Both shape the ecosystem.

questions.prompt: A new highway is built through a forest, cutting it into two separate sections. Over the next decade, the local owl population drops sharply. Which statement best explains this change?
choices: Habitat fragmentation reduced the area owls could hunt and find mates in, disrupting the ecosystem.
Owls evolved to live near roads, so their numbers grew steadily.
Climate change in that decade had no impact on the owl population.
The road acted as a producer that fed the owls.
questions.correctText: Habitat fragmentation reduced the area owls could hunt and find mates in, disrupting the ecosystem.
questions.explanation: Human activities like road building can fragment habitats, leaving smaller areas with fewer resources and isolated populations. Predators that need large territories, like owls, are especially sensitive to this kind of ecosystem disruption.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the relationship between DNA, genes, and traits?
choices: Traits are made of DNA, which is made of cells, which contain genes.
DNA is the chemical that stores information in segments called genes, and genes code for traits like eye color.
Genes are short proteins that wrap around traits to form DNA.
DNA and genes are unrelated; traits come only from the environment.
questions.correctText: DNA is the chemical that stores information in segments called genes, and genes code for traits like eye color.
questions.explanation: DNA is a long molecule found in the nucleus of cells. Specific sections of DNA called genes contain the instructions for traits such as hair color, eye color, and many other characteristics.

questions.prompt: In pea plants, the allele for purple flowers (P) is dominant over the allele for white flowers (p). A Punnett square is made by crossing two heterozygous purple-flowered plants (Bb-style: Pp x Pp). What is the expected ratio of purple-flowered to white-flowered offspring?
choices: All purple, 0 white
3 purple : 1 white
1 purple : 1 white
All white, 0 purple
questions.correctText: 3 purple : 1 white
questions.explanation: The Punnett square for Pp x Pp yields PP, Pp, Pp, pp. Three of the four boxes have at least one dominant P (purple), and one box is pp (white). The expected phenotype ratio is 3 purple : 1 white.

questions.prompt: Within a single litter of puppies from the same two parents, one puppy is much larger than its siblings, one has a slightly different coat pattern, and another has unusually long ears. Which statement best explains these differences?
choices: The puppies cannot be from the same parents, because siblings are always identical.
Sexual reproduction shuffles the parents' genes in new combinations, producing variation within a species.
The puppies' traits will change daily because traits are not inherited.
Only the environment, not genetics, causes variation in offspring.
questions.correctText: Sexual reproduction shuffles the parents' genes in new combinations, producing variation within a species.
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction combines genes from two parents in unique combinations each time, so siblings share many traits but are not identical (unless they are identical twins). This genetic variation is a major source of differences within a species.

questions.prompt: A student finds a rock that has visible layers and contains fossils of small shells. Which type of rock is this most likely to be, and how did it most likely form?
choices: Igneous rock, formed when lava cooled quickly at the surface.
Metamorphic rock, formed when extreme heat and pressure changed an existing rock.
Sedimentary rock, formed when layers of sediment and remains were compacted and cemented over time.
A meteorite, formed in outer space and falling to Earth.
questions.correctText: Sedimentary rock, formed when layers of sediment and remains were compacted and cemented over time.
questions.explanation: Sedimentary rocks form from layers of sediment, often including the remains of organisms. Because they form in layers (strata) at relatively low temperatures, they are the rock type most likely to preserve fossils.

questions.prompt: A map shows two tectonic plates moving directly toward each other, with one plate slowly sinking beneath the other and a chain of volcanoes forming along the edge. Which type of plate boundary is this?
choices: Divergent boundary, where plates move apart.
Transform boundary, where plates slide past each other.
Convergent boundary with subduction, where one plate dives beneath the other.
Stationary boundary, where plates never move.
questions.correctText: Convergent boundary with subduction, where one plate dives beneath the other.
questions.explanation: At convergent boundaries, plates collide. When an oceanic plate meets a continental plate, the denser oceanic plate subducts (sinks) below, melting and producing chains of volcanoes like the Andes or the Pacific 'Ring of Fire.'

questions.prompt: A region sits along the boundary where two tectonic plates suddenly slip past each other. Which natural hazard is this region MOST likely to experience as a direct result of that plate motion?
choices: Hurricanes, because of warm ocean water.
Earthquakes, because stress released at the fault sends seismic waves through the ground.
Tornadoes, because of clashing air masses.
Tides, because of the gravitational pull of the Moon.
questions.correctText: Earthquakes, because stress released at the fault sends seismic waves through the ground.
questions.explanation: When plates suddenly slip past each other along a fault, the built-up stress releases as seismic waves, which we feel as an earthquake. Volcanoes are also common at convergent and divergent boundaries, but earthquakes are the hallmark of transform (sliding) faults.

questions.prompt: After a heavy rainstorm, a hill loses some of its topsoil, which is carried downhill by water and deposited at the bottom of a nearby stream as a small fan-shaped pile. Which sequence of processes best describes what happened?
choices: Deposition -> erosion -> weathering
Weathering -> erosion -> deposition
Erosion -> weathering -> condensation
Subduction -> weathering -> deposition
questions.correctText: Weathering -> erosion -> deposition
questions.explanation: Weathering breaks rock and soil into smaller pieces. Erosion is the movement of those pieces by water, wind, or ice. Deposition is the dropping of the material in a new location. The sequence is weathering -> erosion -> deposition.

questions.topic: Earth and Space Sciences: Weather and Climate
questions.prompt: A puddle in the schoolyard slowly shrinks on a sunny day and disappears by lunch. Later in the week, clouds form, and it rains. Which two steps of the water cycle do these observations represent, in order?
choices: Condensation, then evaporation.
Evaporation, then condensation and precipitation.
Precipitation, then runoff.
Runoff, then transpiration.
questions.correctText: Evaporation, then condensation and precipitation.
questions.explanation: Sunlight provides energy that turns liquid water in the puddle into water vapor (evaporation). When that vapor rises and cools, it condenses into cloud droplets (condensation), which can then fall as rain (precipitation).

questions.topic: Earth and Space Sciences: Weather and Climate
questions.prompt: Earth's atmosphere is made up of several layers. In which layer does most of our weather occur, and which layer contains the ozone that absorbs ultraviolet radiation?
choices: Weather occurs in the stratosphere; ozone is in the troposphere.
Weather occurs in the troposphere; the ozone layer is in the stratosphere.
Weather and ozone are both in the thermosphere.
Weather occurs in the mesosphere; ozone is in the exosphere.
questions.correctText: Weather occurs in the troposphere; the ozone layer is in the stratosphere.
questions.explanation: The troposphere is the lowest layer, where clouds form and weather happens. Above it, the stratosphere contains the ozone layer, which absorbs harmful ultraviolet radiation from the Sun.

questions.topic: Earth and Space Sciences: Weather and Climate
questions.prompt: A meteorologist looks at a weather map and notices the air pressure has been dropping steadily over the last several hours. Which forecast change is MOST consistent with falling air pressure?
choices: Clear skies and sunny weather are very likely.
Stormy weather with clouds and possible precipitation is likely.
An immediate drought will begin.
Air temperature will drop to absolute zero.
questions.correctText: Stormy weather with clouds and possible precipitation is likely.
questions.explanation: Low and falling air pressure usually means rising air, which cools and condenses to form clouds and precipitation. Barometers that drop quickly often signal an approaching storm system. High pressure usually brings clear, fair weather.

questions.topic: Earth and Space Sciences: Weather and Climate
questions.prompt: A student says, 'It snowed yesterday in our town, so I guess our region is not really warming up the way scientists say.' Which response best explains the difference between weather and climate?
choices: The student is correct; one snowy day means there is no long-term warming.
Weather is the long-term average; climate is what is happening right now, so the student is right.
Weather is short-term conditions in one place; climate is the long-term average over decades, so one snowy day does not disprove climate change.
Weather and climate are the same thing measured with different instruments.
questions.correctText: Weather is short-term conditions in one place; climate is the long-term average over decades, so one snowy day does not disprove climate change.
questions.explanation: Weather is the day-to-day state of the atmosphere; climate is the average weather of a region measured over decades. A single cold day or snowstorm does not contradict a long-term warming trend in the climate.

questions.prompt: During a total solar eclipse, observers on a small section of Earth see the Sun briefly blocked. Which alignment of the Sun, Moon, and Earth produces a solar eclipse?
choices: Earth is between the Sun and the Moon, with Earth's shadow falling on the Moon.
The Moon is between the Sun and Earth, with the Moon's shadow falling on Earth.
The Sun is between Earth and the Moon, blocking the Moon's light.
The Sun, Moon, and Earth are at right angles to each other.
questions.correctText: The Moon is between the Sun and Earth, with the Moon's shadow falling on Earth.
questions.explanation: A solar eclipse happens when the Moon passes directly between the Sun and Earth and its shadow lands on a part of Earth. (A lunar eclipse is the reverse: Earth is between the Sun and Moon, with Earth's shadow on the Moon.)

questions.prompt: Earth has seasons mainly because of one key feature of how it orbits the Sun. Which statement best explains why the Northern Hemisphere has summer in June and winter in December?
choices: Earth is much closer to the Sun in June than in December.
Earth's axis is tilted, so the Northern Hemisphere receives more direct sunlight and longer days in June.
The Sun gives off more energy in June than in December.
Earth stops rotating in winter and rotates only in summer.
questions.correctText: Earth's axis is tilted, so the Northern Hemisphere receives more direct sunlight and longer days in June.
questions.explanation: Earth's axis is tilted about 23.5 degrees. In June, the Northern Hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, getting more direct sunlight and longer days (summer). In December, it tilts away, producing winter. Distance from the Sun is almost the same year-round.

questions.prompt: A student reads that our solar system is just a small part of a much larger system called the Milky Way Galaxy. Which statement correctly compares a solar system and a galaxy?
choices: A galaxy is smaller than a solar system and orbits around a planet.
A solar system is one star with the objects orbiting it; a galaxy is billions of stars (with their solar systems) held together by gravity.
A solar system and a galaxy are the same size, just with different names.
A galaxy is a single star, while a solar system contains many stars.
questions.correctText: A solar system is one star with the objects orbiting it; a galaxy is billions of stars (with their solar systems) held together by gravity.
questions.explanation: A solar system is a single star and the planets, moons, and other objects that orbit it. A galaxy is a huge collection of billions of stars, plus gas, dust, and dark matter, all held together by gravity. The Milky Way contains our solar system.

questions.prompt: Scientists describe the Big Bang as the leading explanation for how our universe began about 13.8 billion years ago. Which observation gives the BEST evidence that the universe started in a hot, dense state and has been expanding ever since?
choices: Astronauts can see Earth from the Moon.
Distant galaxies are observed to be moving away from us, and the light from them is shifted toward longer (redder) wavelengths.
The Sun gives off heat and light during the day.
Different planets have different numbers of moons.
questions.correctText: Distant galaxies are observed to be moving away from us, and the light from them is shifted toward longer (redder) wavelengths.
questions.explanation: Observations show that almost all distant galaxies are moving away from us (their light is redshifted), and the farther they are, the faster they are receding. This is strong evidence that space itself has been expanding from a much smaller, hotter beginning, the Big Bang.
